last-assistant-message:
I found and cloned open-source tools into:

[C:\Users\sushi\Documents\Github\resume-ats-tools](C:/Users/sushi/Documents/Github/resume-ats-tools)

**Cloned Tools**
| Tool | Status | What It Tests |
|---|---:|---|
| [ResumeParser](https://github.com/saraprettyman/ResumeParser) | Ran | ATS text readability, skills, JD alignment |
| [ATS-Checker](https://github.com/fuchenru/ATS-Checker) | Ran parser | Basic PDF text extraction / ATS readability |
| [ats-screener](https://github.com/sunnypatell/ats-screener) | Ran local scorer | Simulated Workday, Taleo, iCIMS, Greenhouse, Lever, SuccessFactors |
| [Resume-Matcher](https://github.com/srbhr/Resume-Matcher) | Cloned, not fully run | Full app, JD tailoring, AI suggestions; heavier backend/frontend setup |

I used a generic SWE intern/new-grad JD here:

[C:\Users\sushi\Documents\Github\resume-ats-tools\software_engineering_intern_jd.txt](C:/Users/sushi/Documents/Github/resume-ats-tools/software_engineering_intern_jd.txt)

**ResumeParser Results**
Your resume matched most core SWE stack keywords:
- Matched: `Python`, `Java`, `Kotlin`, `JavaScript`, `TypeScript`, `React`, `Vue.js`, `Node.js`, `Flask`, `AWS`, `Docker`, `Kubernetes`, `Terraform`, `PostgreSQL`, `Git`
- Missing/weak against generic SWE intern JD: `Testing`, `CI/CD`, `Agile`

Important nuance: you *do* mention CI/CD and Agile conceptually, but the tool still flagged them. That means exact wording/section placement matters.

Profile parser also showed an ATS-readability issue: some extracted text collapsed spaces, like `JuniorSoftwareDeveloper`, `UniversityofAlberta`, and dense bullet text. That suggests your PDF formatting is mostly readable but not perfect for stricter parsers.

**ats-screener Scores**
Using the generic SWE intern/new-grad JD:

| Simulated ATS | Score | Pass |
|---|---:|---:|
| Workday | 69 | No |
| Taleo | 66 | Yes |
| SuccessFactors | 66 | Yes |
| iCIMS | 71 | Yes |
| Greenhouse | 86 | Yes |
| Lever | 74 | Yes |

Take this with a grain of salt: I had to run the scorer from extracted text, and its bullet detection still undercounted your experience bullets. But the direction is useful.

**Main Findings**
Your resume is strongest for human-reviewed systems like Greenhouse/Lever-style flows. It does worse on stricter keyword systems like Workday/Taleo because the generic JD has exact words your resume doesn’t use enough:
- `testing`
- `collaboration`
- `code reviews`
- `debugging`
- `relational databases`
- `data structures`
- `algorithms`
- `frontend`
- `cloud infrastructure`
- `maintainable code`

Best resume fixes for ATS-style tools:
- Add a visible `Contact` heading or make contact info parse more clearly.
- Add `Testing`, `CI/CD`, `Agile/Scrum`, `Code Reviews`, and `Debugging` explicitly in Skills or Experience.
- Add `SQL` or `Relational Databases` next to PostgreSQL/Aurora.
- Add `Data Structures & Algorithms` if targeting intern/new-grad roles.
- Improve PDF spacing so metrics don’t become `by89%` or `Mentored500+students`.
- Keep a simple one-column version for applications, even if your prettier version looks better.

The most useful next test is to run these against 2-3 real postings you care about, because role-specific scoring changes a lot by JD.
